equations.name: SEIR model — dS/dt
equations.notes: Pure multiplicative interaction; all operations elementary

equations.name: SEIR model — dE/dt
equations.notes: Reuses βSI term; sigma*E adds 2n, subtraction adds 2n

equations.name: SEIR model — dI/dt
equations.notes: Two linear decay/growth terms minus each other

equations.name: SEIR model — full per-step cost
equations.notes: Full SEIR step cost; all four compartments evaluated simultaneously

equations.name: Basic reproduction number
equations.notes: Key threshold parameter; purely rational in model parameters

equations.name: Herd immunity threshold
equations.notes: Derived from R0; fraction of population needing immunity

equations.name: Doubling time
equations.notes: ln(2) is a fixed constant; only the division is runtime cost

equations.name: Logistic epidemic curve
equations.notes: Sigmoid growth; fully closed-form; no special functions required

equations.name: Serial interval distribution (gamma distribution PDF)
equations.notes: The Gamma function Gamma(alpha) for non-integer alpha is non-elementary (transcends ELC). All other operations are INSIDE ELC. The ELC boundary passes directly through this distribution: the exponential and power terms are fine, but Gamma is not.

equations.name: Vaccine efficacy
equations.notes: ARV = attack rate vaccinated, ARU = attack rate unvaccinated

equations.name: Force of infection
equations.notes: Per-capita infection pressure; rational combination of parameters

equations.name: Effective reproduction number
equations.notes: Time-varying version of R0; tracks epidemic trajectory in real time

summary.boundary_case: Serial interval gamma distribution PDF — Gamma(alpha) for non-integer alpha is non-elementary

key_finding.title: Deterministic/stochastic boundary in epidemiology
key_finding.description: Deterministic compartmental models (SIR, SEIR) and closed-form summary statistics (R0, Rt, herd immunity, VE, CFR, doubling time, logistic curve) are entirely INSIDE ELC. The ELC boundary appears precisely at stochastic/distributional models that require the Gamma function for non-integer shape parameters. The serial interval distribution is the canonical example: its exponential and power-law components are ELC-valid, but Gamma(alpha) for real non-integer alpha is non-elementary.
key_finding.implication: ELC separates deterministic epidemic dynamics from distributional epidemic statistics. Moment-based approximations (using integer-alpha Gamma, which reduces to factorials) would restore ELC membership.
key_finding.ELC_outside_reason: Gamma function is analytic but non-elementary — it cannot be expressed as a finite composition of SuperBEST v5 operators for general non-integer arguments
